perturbation.original_text: 2.1 "Commercial Rights" means any and all rights of a commercial nature connected with the Arena, including image rights, broadcasting rights, new media rights, endorsement and official supplier rights, sponsorship rights, merchandising rights, licensing rights, advertising rights, hospitality rights and all intellectual property rights in and to the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 "Commercial Rights" means some rights of a commercial nature connected with the Arena, including image rights, broadcasting rights, new media rights, endorsement and official supplier rights, sponsorship rights, merchandising rights, licensing rights, advertising rights, hospitality rights and intellectual property rights at the discretion of Allied Esports International, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “any and all rights” to “some rights” and adding “at the discretion of Allied Esports International, Inc.,” the definition of Commercial Rights becomes subjective and potentially undermines the enforceability of rights granted to Newegg, contradicting the original intent of clearly defined commercial rights.
perturbation.law_explanation: California law requires mutual consent and clear, unambiguous terms in a contract. Modifying the definition of 'Commercial Rights' to be discretionary introduces ambiguity, potentially invalidating the contract due to a lack of definite terms.  This makes the agreement uncertain and unenforceable, as it is not clear which commercial rights Newegg actually receives.

OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 1. NATURE OF A CONTRACT [1549 - 1615] ( Title 1 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 3. Consent [1565 - 1590] ( Chapter 3 enacted 1872. ) 1565. The consent of the parties to a contract must be: 1. Free; 2. Mutual; and, 3. Communicated by each to the other. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The change from "any and all rights" to "some rights" coupled with the addition of "at the discretion of Allied Esports International, Inc." directly contradicts the requirement for mutual consent and clear, unambiguous terms in a contract, as explained in the Law Explanation. The modification introduces ambiguity, potentially invalidating the contract due to a lack of definite terms, making the agreement uncertain and unenforceable. Scraped Law Snippet 1, California Code, CIV 1565, states that the consent of the parties to a contract must be mutual. The modified text gives Allied Esports International, Inc. discretionary power over Commercial Rights, which undermines the mutuality requirement because the rights Newegg receives are no longer clearly defined and agreed upon but subject to the discretion of another party.

perturbation.original_text: 15.1 Relationship. The relationship of the Parties is solely that of independent contractors, and each Party will represent itself to any third parties only as such. Neither Party has the power to bind, represent or act for the other Party. The Parties have no agency, partnership, joint venture or fiduciary duties to each other.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.1 Relationship. The relationship of the Parties is that of independent contractors. Allied will act as Newegg's agent for esports marketing purposes. Neither Party has the power to bind or represent the other Party except as related to event marketing. The Parties have no partnership, joint venture or fiduciary duties to each other.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that Allied will act as Newegg's agent for esports marketing purposes, the contract creates an agency relationship that contradicts the initial statement that the parties are independent contractors, creating uncertainty on who is liable in the event of breaches.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code Section 2317 defines agency, and introducing agency language creates a contradiction. If Allied acts with 'apparent authority,' Newegg could be bound by Allied's actions, which conflicts with the clause stating neither party can bind the other. This change subjects Newegg to potential liabilities arising from Allied's actions in esports marketing, counter to the original independent contractor agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 2317. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 4. OBLIGATIONS ARISING FROM PARTICULAR TRANSACTIONS [1738 - 3273.69] ( Part 4 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 9. AGENCY [2295 - 2400] ( Title 9 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 1. Agency in General [2295 - 2357] ( Chapter 1 enacted 1872. ) ARTICLE 2. Authority of Agents [2304 - 2326] ( Article 2 enacted 1872. ) 2317. Ostensible authority is such as a principal, intentionally or by want of ordinary care, causes or allows a third person to believe the agent to possess. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an agency relationship between Allied and Newegg for esports marketing, which directly contradicts the original statement establishing them as independent contractors. This misalignment, as highlighted in the perturbation explanation, creates uncertainty regarding liability. The Law Explanation specifies California Civil Code Section 2317 defines agency, and Allied acting with apparent authority could bind Newegg, which conflicts with the original clause stating neither party can bind the other. Scraped Law Snippet 1, citing California Civil Code Section 2317, defines 'Ostensible authority' as actions by a principal (Newegg) that lead a third party to believe Allied is their agent. This directly supports the claim that the modified text creates a contradiction by potentially granting Allied agency power that the original contract explicitly denies.

perturbation.original_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. Without reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as the exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California. Before any Party (the "Complaining Party") may bring any legal proceeding against the other (the "Non Complaining Party"), the Complaining Party shall first make a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve all disputes privately by notifying and providing to the Non Complaining Party of the Complaining Party's complaints, reasons and supporting evidence for the complaints, and the reasonable steps Complaining Party would like the Non Complaining Party to take in order to address the complaints. If for any reason the Non-Complaining Party disagrees with either the complaint or the steps suggested to address the complaints, the Parties shall discuss and work on an amicable solution for at least thirty (30) days before the Complaining Party may bring any legal proceeding to resolve the complaints. Any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope and applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles County, California, by an arbitrator of JAMS, in accordance with its arbitration rules and procedures then in effect. Judgment on the arbitrator's award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The prevailing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party its costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees (including any fees for expert witnesses, paralegals, or other legal service providers). This Section 15.10 shall not preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. Without reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to non-exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as a possible venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California. Before any Party (the "Complaining Party") may bring any legal proceeding against the other (the "Non Complaining Party"), the Complaining Party shall first make a reasonable attempt to resolve all disputes privately by notifying and providing to the Non Complaining Party of the Complaining Party's complaints, reasons and some evidence for the complaints, and the possible steps Complaining Party would like the Non Complaining Party to take in order to address the complaints. If for any reason the Non-Complaining Party may or may not agree with either the complaint or the steps suggested to address the complaints, the Parties may or may not discuss and work on a possible solution for possibly thirty (30) days before the Complaining Party may bring any legal proceeding to resolve the complaints. Any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope and applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, may be determined by arbitration in another county, California, by an arbitrator of possibly JAMS, in accordance with its arbitration rules and procedures then in effect. Judgment on the arbitrator's award may not be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The losing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall possibly be entitled to recover from the other Party the fraction of expenses and attorneys' fees (possibly including any fees for legal service providers). This Section 15.10 may preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the language concerning jurisdiction and arbitration introduces substantial ambiguity and makes the dispute resolution process uncertain, which undermines the purpose of having a clear governing law clause.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Federal Arbitration Act (9 U.S.C. § 2) favors the enforcement of arbitration agreements. By making the arbitration location and method uncertain ('another county, California, by an arbitrator of possibly JAMS'), limiting the recovery of attorneys' fees ('fraction of expenses'), and possibly precluding a Party from seeking injunctive relief, the modification undermines the enforceability and clarity of the arbitration clause, contradicting the FAA's intent to promote and enforce arbitration agreements.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and uncertainty in the dispute resolution process, contradicting the Federal Arbitration Act's (FAA) intent to promote and enforce arbitration agreements. The original text had clear language regarding jurisdiction, venue, and arbitration, whereas the changed text uses terms like 'non-exclusive jurisdiction,' 'possible venue,' 'possibly thirty (30) days,' 'another county,' and 'possibly JAMS,' which weakens the enforceability of the agreement. The Law Snippets, specifically 9 U.S. Code § 2, emphasize the 'validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate.' The modifications in the changed text directly undermine this principle by introducing uncertainty, making the arbitration agreement less enforceable and creating a contradiction.
